sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Reports recurrent lung infections and concerns about pneumonia.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports experiencing recurrent lung infections over the past six months, particularly when unloading hay. He describes symptoms including a cough, shortness of breath, and a low-grade fever. He has sought treatment from his primary care physician and urgent care facilities, receiving antibiotics. He notes that his symptoms typically last for a few days to a week. He has been helping his brother with farm work, including unloading hay, and suspects this may be related to his symptoms. He denies any personal history of allergies but inquires about family history. He reports no smoking and does not live with anyone who smokes. He also reports a history of frequent pneumonia episodes.
Findings
The patient's vital signs reveal a respiratory rate of 20 breaths per minute and oxygen saturation of 99% on room air. Lung exam reveals fine rales in the lower lung fields but no wheezes. Physical examination does not reveal clubbing of the fingers. Chest X-ray demonstrates round glass opacities in the lungs.
Assessment
The patient's symptoms and history are consistent with hypersensitivity pneumonitis (farmer's lung), a condition frequently associated with occupational exposure to allergens. A history of recurrent pneumonia episodes further supports this diagnosis.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I am prescribing an oral steroid to reduce inflammation in the lungs. I am ordering a cat scan of the lungs to confirm the diagnosis and a pulmonary function test to assess respiratory impairment. I recommend wearing a respirator when unloading hay to prevent further exposure to allergens. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess response to treatment and adjust the plan as needed.
